content.problemStatement:
Chefland is a country with NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 bidirectional roads. It is possible to travel from each city to any other city using the roads. Moreover, each city is specialised in either black or white chocolate.

Chef wants to construct three new restaurants. However, he is still not sure where to build them. You should answer QQQ queries; in each query, Chef gives you two cities LLL and RRR and you should find the number of ways to choose a set of three distinct cities that satisfies the following conditions:


The chosen cities belong to the shortest path between the cities LLL and RRR (both inclusive).
For any two chosen cities (let's denote them by uuu and vvv; u‚âÝvu \neq vuÓÄÝ=v), there is at least one city specialised in black chocolate on the shortest path between the cities uuu and vvv (both inclusive).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. For each valid iii, Ai=1A_i = 1Ai‚Äã=1 if the iii-th city is specialised in black chocolate or 000 if it is specialised in white chocolate.
Each of the following N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that cities xxx and yyy are connected by a road.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of ways to choose three cities.
